steps.title: Identify the angle at line r
steps.content: Where transversal t crosses line r, we have a 107° angle positioned above line r on the left side of the transversal.

steps.title: Apply corresponding angles with parallel lines
steps.content:
Since lines r and s are parallel, the corresponding angles theorem applies: angles in the same relative position at each intersection are equal. The angle x° at line s is above line s on the left side of transversal t—the same position as the 107° angle at line r.
$$x° = 107°$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
